---
title: "Reflection"
---

Creating this ePortfolio has been one of the most valuable learning experiences in this module, as it required me to combine technical skills, organisation, creativity, and critical self-reflection.

## What I Learned

Through this project, I developed practical skills in **Quarto, Git, GitHub, Python, and web design**. I learned how to structure a professional website, organise files effectively, manage changes using version control, and present work in a clear and visually engaging way.

## Challenges I Faced

One of the main challenges was understanding how all the technical components of the portfolio worked together. I had to learn how Quarto uses file structure, page rendering, and YAML configuration to control navigation and layout.

I also encountered issues with broken links, page structure, Python package setup, and rendering project pages correctly in Quarto.

## How I Solved Problems

I approached these challenges by working through them step by step. I tested changes, corrected file paths, improved my Quarto configuration, and installed the required Python packages.

This process showed me that technical work often involves **debugging, testing, and refining solutions**, rather than expecting everything to work immediately.


::: {.callout-important}
A key lesson from this project is that professional digital work is not only about writing content — it also involves **testing, debugging, refining, and presenting work effectively**.
:::

## Skills Development

This project strengthened my confidence in:
- building and styling a website using Quarto  
- managing a project using Git and GitHub  
- using Python for data analysis and visualisation  
- presenting academic work in a clear and professional format  

## Challenge Example

One specific challenge I faced was configuring Python within Quarto. Missing dependencies caused errors during rendering, which prevented my project pages from working correctly.

I resolved this by installing the required packages and ensuring the Python environment was correctly configured. This improved my troubleshooting ability and gave me a better understanding of how development environments function.

## Future Application

The skills developed in this project will be valuable in both academic and professional contexts. In the future, I aim to build more advanced data analysis projects and continue improving how I communicate technical work.

This experience has increased my confidence in using digital tools not only to complete assignments, but also to create work that is **polished, purposeful, and professionally presented**.

These skills are directly applicable to a future role in data analysis, where structuring data, debugging workflows, and communicating insights are essential.

## Development Process

The diagram below illustrates the structured workflow followed in developing this ePortfolio.

```{mermaid}
%%{init: {'flowchart': {'useMaxWidth': true}}}%%
graph LR
A[Initial Planning] --> B[Building Pages]
B --> C[Adding Projects]
C --> D[Testing & Debugging]
D --> E[Styling]
E --> F[Final Reflection]
```
## Additional Learning Resource

The video below provides an overview of version control using Git and GitHub, supporting the concepts applied throughout the development of this ePortfolio.

{{< video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RGOj5yH7evk >}}

You can also view the video directly here:  
[Git and GitHub for Beginners](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RGOj5yH7evk)


## References

These resources supported my learning during the development of this portfolio.

- Programming with Mosh. (2020). *Git and GitHub for Beginners – Crash Course*. YouTube. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RGOj5yH7evk  
- Intro to Data Analysis / Visualization with Python, Matplotlib and Pandas. (2018). YouTube. https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=a9UrKTVEeZA
